claims.claim: Unlike naive lines-of-code (LOC) gates, the budgeter scores diffs on multiple hygiene signals—file count, domain spread, generated/lockfile presence, hunk density, and weighted cost—and emits pass/warn/fail decisions.

claims.claim: The budgeter passed all five predefined acceptance criteria: it accepted 35 of 40 historical commits as pass or warn (12.5% fail rate, within the ≤30% threshold), correctly flagged a scattered 24-file sweep and a lockfile-coupled diff that LOC-only checks passed, and passed a small doc-only control.

claims.claim: These results support viability with caveats: historical accepted commits are proxy positives rather than human-adjudicated hygiene labels, negative controls are synthetic, and thresholds require per-repository calibration with an explicit override path for releases, merge commits, and generated artifact updates.

claims.claim: A diff that touches 24 files across multiple domains with only 48 changed lines is arguably riskier than a diff that touches 2 files with 48 changed lines, yet both pass a LOC-only gate at the same threshold.
